claim: Sub-agents in Claude Code keep main thread context lean by delegating research/implementation to isolated agents that return summaries
actionable: Use /agents command in Claude Code to spawn multiple concurrent sub-agents for research; main thread stays efficient for subsequent tasks
source_title: Claude Code Subagents are Absolutely Insane

claim: Running 5-10 concurrent sub-agents covers codebase more thoroughly than single main-thread search; each agent has fresh context for exploration
actionable: When searching large codebases, spawn multiple sub-agents in parallel; each works independently, reducing coverage gaps
source_title: Claude Code Subagents are Absolutely Insane

claim: Context window grows slower with sub-agents (~16% vs 30% for equivalent task in main thread); sub-agents keep parent thread lean
actionable: Monitor context window growth; if approaching 50% usage, delegate work to sub-agents to reset context before next major task
source_title: Claude Code Subagents are Absolutely Insane

claim: Custom agents (file finder, security scanner, code architect) can be created with specific instructions, tool access levels, and models (Haiku, Opus)
actionable: Create domain-specific agents (researcher, auditor, implementer) with tailored prompts and model choices; reuse across tasks
source_title: Claude Code Subagents are Absolutely Insane

claim: Agents run concurrently; spawning 10 agents reduces response time compared to sequential execution; more agents = more thorough coverage
actionable: Parallelize agent work; expect 2-3x throughput improvement by running agents concurrently instead of sequentially
source_title: Claude Code Subagents are Absolutely Insane

claim: Context window stays at 16% even after spawning 10 security agents; giving agents minimal relevant context improves quality by reducing noise
actionable: Less context is better when agents are focused; provide only the specific files/info relevant to the agent's task
source_title: Claude Code Subagents are Absolutely Insane
